commands.repair_reason: - [P1] Human review should decide the cloud-cron default watchdog compatibility and request or accept live cron proof; no narrow automated code repair is identified.; Cleared: The diff only changes agent timeout resolver logic and its focused test; no dependency, workflow, secret, package, or code-execution supply-chain surface was changed. commands.review_summary:
The branch removes the cron-wide no-timeout return from the embedded LLM idle-timeout resolver and updates focused tests so cron cloud calls get the default watchdog while cron local-provider calls still opt out.

PR surface: Source -4, Tests +6. Total +2 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. at source level: current main returns 0 for resolveLlmIdleTimeoutMs({ trigger: "cron" }) and the production cron path passes trigger "cron" into the embedded runner. I did not run a live packaged cron scenario.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Human review should decide the cloud-cron default watchdog compatibility and request or accept live cron proof; no narrow automated code repair is identified.

target.title: fix(agents): keep cron cloud idle watchdog enabled
target.body:
## Summary

- Fixes cron-triggered embedded agent runs skipping the default LLM idle watchdog when no explicit timeout is configured.
- Keeps the existing timeout precedence intact: explicit run timeout, provider request timeout, and `agents.defaults.timeoutSeconds` still win over the implicit default.
- Preserves the local-provider opt-out so local models can continue long prompt evaluation without being aborted by the cloud-provider silence guard.
- Adds focused regression coverage for both cron cloud/no-timeout behavior and cron local-provider behavior.
- Out of scope: changing lane scheduling, queued-work diagnostics, recovery policy, or the configured timeout model.

Reviewers should focus on whether the cron trigger should share the same cloud-provider idle guard as other embedded runs when no explicit timeout is set, and whether the local-provider exception remains narrow enough.

## Linked context

Maintainer-requested fix after investigation of a cron responsiveness blocker. No public issue is linked.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: Cron-triggered embedded agent runs could enter a cloud model call with no implicit idle watchdog when no explicit timeout was configured.
- Real environment tested: Not rerun as a packaged live cron scenario after this patch.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:
  - `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/llm-idle-timeout.test.ts src/agents/openai-transport-stream.test.ts src/llm/providers/openai-responses-shared.test.ts`
  - `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.core.test.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/core-test.tsbuildinfo`
  - `git diff --check origin/main...HEAD`
  - `codex review --base origin/main`
  - `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode local`
- Evidence after fix: The resolver now returns the default 120s idle watchdog for cron cloud/no-timeout calls, while cron local-provider calls still return `0`.
- Observed result after fix: Focused timeout coverage passed; the two CI-failing OpenAI tool-conversion test files passed after rebasing onto current `origin/main`; the core test typecheck command that failed in CI passed locally after the rebase.
- What was not tested: A packaged live gateway cron reproduction after patch; broad `pnpm check` was not run locally because this is a Codex worktree and the root guide routes broad pnpm-gated proof to Crabbox/Testbox.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: This PR includes focused resolver proof for the changed decision point, not an end-to-end cron runtime recording.
- Before evidence: The previous test expectation documented the old cron-wide opt-out by expecting `0` for `trigger: "cron"` with no timeout configuration.

## Tests and validation

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/llm-idle-timeout.test.ts src/agents/openai-transport-stream.test.ts src/llm/providers/openai-responses-shared.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.core.test.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/core-test.tsbuildinfo`
- `git diff --check origin/main...HEAD`
- `codex review --base origin/main`
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode local`

Regression coverage was updated in `src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/llm-idle-timeout.test.ts` to lock down the new cron cloud default and the retained cron local-provider opt-out.

CI note: the previous failing run was on a stale merge from older `main`; the branch was rebased onto current `origin/main` and force-pushed with only the intended PR commit retained.

## Risk checklist

Did user-visible behavior change? `Yes`

Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? `No`

Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? `No`

Highest-risk area: very slow cloud cron responses with no stream progress may now abort after the default idle window unless an explicit timeout is configured.

Mitigation: existing explicit run/provider/agent timeout precedence remains unchanged, and local-provider calls continue to opt out of the implicit cloud watchdog.

## Current review state

Next action: CI rerun on the rebased branch, then maintainer review.

Still waiting on: fresh CI for commit `0d3d0fb539` and any maintainer-requested broader runtime proof.

Bot/reviewer comments addressed: local Codex review and autoreview reported no accepted/actionable findings.

## AI assistance disclosure

This PR was prepared with AI assistance. The change was reviewed against the touched resolver, its production caller path, focused regression tests, and the relevant embedded-run timeout behavior.
